meta.title: mac.riera.co.uk
meta.description: Opinionated MacBook Pro rebuild guide and Homebrew installer.
meta.tagline: A clean, repeatable Mac setup for Joan Marc Riera's actual daily stack.

groups.label: CLI
groups.description: Core shell and terminal tools for day one.
notes: zsh stays minimal: no autojump and no zsh-syntax-highlighting.

groups.label: Dev
groups.description: Coding and writing tools that belong on every workstation rebuild.
notes: Node.js version management goes through Volta. pnpm is intentionally excluded.
OrbStack is the local container and Linux path. Docker Desktop stays out of the default rebuild.

groups.label: Browsers + AI
groups.description: Browser and coding assistants used in the daily workflow.
notes: Chrome, Chrome Canary, and Vivaldi all stay available because they each cover a different browser role during setup and testing.
Antigravity is part of the current AI toolchain.

groups.label: Productivity
groups.description: Window management, keyboard automation, notes, and personal utilities.
notes: Keyboard Maestro replaces Alfred, Raycast, and standalone clipboard managers.
Keyboard Maestro and Rectangle Pro still require paid licenses after install.
BetterDisplay stays because external monitor scaling and brightness control keep coming up.
MailMate beta is the default on Apple Silicon because the stable cask currently requires Rosetta 2.
KeeWeb was intentionally replaced with KeePassXC because KeeWeb's desktop releases have stalled.

groups.label: Backup
groups.description: Optional backup extras if Time Machine to TrueNAS is not enough.
notes: If Time Machine to the TrueNAS or NFS target already gives a complete backup and clean restore path, skip this group.
Kopia stays optional for cases where an extra encrypted offsite copy or repository-style verification is still useful.

groups.label: Capture
notes: Shottr and Kap stay in the default workstation because they are used often.

groups.label: Network
groups.description: Diagnostics, remote access, and support tooling kept out of the default rebuild.
notes: Useful when the Mac is doubling as a support or network box.
The current Homebrew cask token is tailscale-app, not tailscale.

bundles.label: Minimal
bundles.description: Enough to browse, code, and get back into the repo quickly.

bundles.label: Workstation
bundles.description: The usual day-one rebuild for daily work.

bundles.label: Full
bundles.description: Daily rebuild plus network and support tools.
